name: 25_Speech_Emotion_Recognition_CNN_LSTM_RAVDESS_DL
query: I am seeking a speech emotion recognition project using a CNN-LSTM model with the RAVDESS dataset, which should be downloaded from Kaggle or [this Hugging Face link](https://huggingface.co/datasets/xbgoose/ravdess). The project should load the dataset and perform robust audio preprocessing (noise removal and normalization) and MFCC feature extraction, implemented in `src/data_loader.py`. The CNN-LSTM model should be implemented in `src/model.py`. Recognition accuracy should be saved in `results/metrics/recognition_accuracy.txt`, and a confusion matrix should be generated and saved as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`. Additionally, a user-friendly local API should be created using Flask to allow users to upload audio files and receive emotion recognition results, with the implementation included in `src/hci.py`.

requirements.criteria: The "RAVDESS" dataset is loaded in `src/data_loader.py`, which is downloaded from Kaggle or [this Hugging Face link](https://huggingface.co/datasets/xbgoose/ravdess).
Audio preprocessing, including noise removal and normalization, is implemented in `src/data_loader.py`.
MFCC feature extraction is implemented in `src/data_loader.py`.
The confusion matrix is generated and saved as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.
A local API is created using "Flask" to allow users to upload audio files and receive emotion recognition results. The implementation should be included in `src/hci.py`.

preferences.criteria: The audio preprocessing step should be robust, effectively reducing noise while preserving the integrity of the speech signals.
The local API should be user-friendly, with clear instructions for uploading files and interpreting results.

judge_stats.criteria: The "RAVDESS" dataset is loaded in `src/data_loader.py`, which is downloaded from Kaggle or [this Hugging Face link](https://huggingface.co/datasets/xbgoose/ravdess).

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/data_loader.py` does not actually load the RAVDESS dataset from Kaggle or the cited Hugging Face link. The file only contains placeholder text in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` mentioning `"RAVDESS"` and a stub `load_dataset()` that returns `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}`. There is no code for downloading, reading audio files, using Kaggle/Hugging Face APIs, or accessing dataset paths.

judge_stats.criteria: Audio preprocessing, including noise removal and normalization, is implemented in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: In `src/data_loader.py`, there is no actual audio preprocessing implementation. The file only contains placeholder functions: `load_dataset()` returns `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}`, and `build_transforms()` returns a static list `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]`. There is no code for loading audio, removing noise, or performing normalization on audio data. The requirement is merely mentioned as text in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, not implemented.

judge_stats.criteria: MFCC feature extraction is implemented in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/data_loader.py` does not implement MFCC extraction. The file only contains placeholder functions like `load_dataset()` returning `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and `build_transforms()` returning `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]`. Although the string `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` mentions “MFCC feature extraction,” there is no actual MFCC code, no audio processing library usage (e.g. librosa), and no feature extraction function in the file.

<UNSATISFIED>: `src/model.py` does not implement a CNN-LSTM model. The file only contains metadata-like strings (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`) and a trivial `build_model()` function that returns `"model placeholder"` rather than defining any CNN, LSTM, or neural network architecture. No imports from PyTorch/TensorFlow or model layer definitions appear in the code.

<UNSATISFIED>: Although `results/metrics/recognition_accuracy.txt` exists, its content is `('placeholder\r\n', None)`, which is a placeholder rather than a saved recognition accuracy value. No evidence shows an actual numeric accuracy metric was written to this file.

judge_stats.criteria: The confusion matrix is generated and saved as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: Although a file exists at `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png` in the workspace tree, the provided evidence shows it is not a valid readable image: the file content check returned `Error reading image file ... "The image data you provided does not represent a valid image."` There are also no trajectory or execution logs confirming successful generation or saving of the confusion matrix.

judge_stats.criteria: A local API is created using "Flask" to allow users to upload audio files and receive emotion recognition results. The implementation should be included in `src/hci.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: `src/hci.py` does not implement a Flask API. The file only contains a benchmark placeholder with `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS = ["Flask", "lstm"]`, and a `main()` that returns a static dictionary. There is no `from flask import Flask`, no app/routes, no audio upload handling, and no code returning emotion recognition results. No execution evidence or outputs demonstrate a working local API.
